Tests regular expression and integer parsing functionality.

# Purpose
This code snippet demonstrates the use of two JavaScript functions: `RegExp` and `parseInt`. The `RegExp` constructor creates a new regular expression object with the pattern `"m\u2820"`, where `\u2820` represents a Unicode character. The `parseInt` function converts the string `"145"` into an integer, resulting in the number 145. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on the creation of a regular expression and the conversion of a string to an integer.
